title: ID Agents Control Center Wiki
description: Human-readable wiki and editable UI schematic for the Control Center. Each page.body field is the prose wiki article; the desktop app still reads page nav metadata for sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility, and keeps the structured sections as supporting implementation detail.

editContract.hotReload: The desktop app polls this file while running. Local edits to body prose, nav labels, icons, order, visibility, page descriptions, tabs, tiles, fields, dropdowns, controls, displays, and routing notes appear in the UI without a rebuild.
editContract.pathOverride: Set IDCTL_WIKI_PATH=/absolute/path/to/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json to drive the UI from a different local schematic file.
editContract.routeRule: A page only becomes navigable if its route is implemented in idctl-desktop/src/renderer/App.tsx. Unknown routes are shown in the Wiki but skipped by the sidebar.
editContract.body: pages[].body is markdown prose for operators. Keep each article grounded in current renderer behavior, explain when to use the page, and call out routing or safety boundaries. Do not remove nav, route, component, sourceFiles, or structured sections because those continue to drive the live UI and schematic detail.
editContract.releaseDrift: scripts/check-wiki.mjs compares this appVersion and updated marker with idctl-desktop/package.json, validates page sourceFiles, and fails when a page source file changes without docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json changing in the same worktree diff. Set IDCTL_WIKI_DRIFT_BASE=<ref> to compare committed branch changes against a base ref as well.
editContract.releaseNaming: Operator-facing release notes, ops/deployer handoffs, and ops-team content-ops release-cycle wiki updates should say IDACC release / idacc release, not IACC release / iacc release. IDACC means ID Agent Control Center.

global.shell: Electron desktop app. Renderer calls window.idagents.call(method, ...args); preload sends IPC to main; main either handles app-local methods or forwards manager methods through bridge.ts and ManagerClient.
global.state: useFleet in src/renderer/store.ts keeps the live snapshot: connection, manager URL, active team, coordinator, agents, teams, events, inbox, unread chat count, all-team agents, and refresh functions. Renderer mutations also emit coarse sync-domain invalidations so mounted Dashboard, Work, Inbox, Projects, and Health views refresh together after plans, goals, loops, schedules, tasks, project, org, and agent-capability changes. The fleet primary lead is locked to the default team; other teams can still choose coordinators that the default primary delegates to and default-team validators review. The external Brain dashboard's /fleet-report now mirrors IDACC's all-team manager polling contract: /teams, then /agents per team, with Brain-cache fallback and drift warnings instead of stale cached counts.

pages.body:
# Dashboard

Dashboard is the daily command view. It combines lead chat for the selected team with all-team coordination, active task state, recent events, and hierarchy context so an operator can steer work without losing fleet awareness.

## Use it for

- Send a request to the selected team's lead while watching coordination across every running team.
- Check whether work is moving through the leadership chain before opening the deeper Work or Health pages.
- Read the event stream when you need fast context on what just changed.

## Current guardrails

Dashboard is now observe-first by default. Command-palette project and org sync entries open Projects or HR Manager instead of mutating tracker or hierarchy state immediately. The shortcuts drawer shows Probe all, Refresh, and owner-page handoffs first; direct project sync and org sync are kept under Advanced direct shortcuts, and direct org sync previews affected agents, goal files, brain writes, planned rebuilds, and deferred busy rebuilds before apply. Primary fleet-lead changes are no longer exposed as a Dashboard selector; use HR Manager for the routing diff preview.

## Watch for

The Dashboard is not the fleet roster grid. Agent lifecycle controls, probes, and full roster inspection live on Health through AgentTable. If the question is "who is running?" use Health; if the question is "what should the lead do next?" start here.

pages.body:
# Chat

Chat is the full conversation workspace behind Dashboard lead chat. It owns sessions, target selection, project focus, attachments, generated images, live traces, resumable polling, unread state, and saved history.

## Use it for

- Continue or inspect a conversation with complete message history.
- Attach files, paste context, generate image context, or focus the dispatch on a tracked project.
- Review the behind-the-scenes trace for tool, file, and delegation activity after a reply lands.

## Routing note

Chat is currently embedded into Dashboard and hidden from normal sidebar navigation. Dashboard uses the same dispatch path but locks the target to the selected team's coordinator; direct Chat use is for contextual or deep-link flows that need the full session surface.

pages.body:
# Inbox

Inbox is the operator decision queue. It separates direct blocker questions and manager inbox items from the general event feed so work that needs a human answer is visible and actionable.

## Use it for

- Answer option questions surfaced by agents or Work blocker scans.
- Reply to manager inbox items that are explicitly waiting on the operator.
- Dismiss stale prompts after deciding they no longer need a response.

## Operating rule

Use Inbox to resolve blockers, then return to Chat or Work for continued execution. It is intentionally narrower than a conversation view: the goal is to clear pending decisions and update the linked task state where the manager provides a task reference.

pages.body:
# Work

Work is the execution surface for plans, task cards, schedules, loops, and dream review. It turns operator intent into tracked manager work and gives the team a place to move items through planning, assignment, review, and completion.

## Start here when

- A request needs to become tasks, not just a chat reply.
- You need to triage unassigned work, assign a fleet objective, or surface blockers for a human decision.
- You want scheduled follow-up, recurring loops, or reviewed idea drafts.

## Tab guide

- Plans: structured plans and dispatch handoff.
- Tasks: Kanban execution, review lanes, scoped assignment, and blocker surfacing.
- Schedule: team-aware heartbeats and supervision reminders.
- Loops: recurring objectives.
- Dream: proposed ideas or simulations that require review before action.

Dragging cards now fresh-reads the task, lane overlay, and dependency overlay before it writes a review lane or mapped manager status. Assignment and re-dispatch also fresh-read live status, owner, and team before overwriting ownership or sending another background request. Plans now fresh-read live brain-plan title/status/mtime before status writes, blocker questions, or dispatch, and draft-plan update/promote/delete paths block if the opened draft changed. Goals now fresh-read the opened goal before field edits, AI refine saves, and delete; the goal driver merges metadata into the latest saved goal and Autopilot prompts show global active-goal scope. Loops now keep a baseline for saved chains and block stale save, run, schedule, or delete actions before overwriting a newer chain. Schedule now fresh-reads heartbeat and check-in rows before update, pause, remove, close, or bulk cleanup. Dream prevents duplicate nightly schedules for the same team/agent and fresh-checks reports before delete. Assignment can dispatch to one team, selected teams, team leads, or every active agent, grouping manager plan creation by target team so owners stay scoped correctly. Schedule heartbeats use team-qualified targets, so cross-team agents and manager-level heartbeats remain visible and editable from the Schedule tab.

pages.body:
# Projects

Projects is the local workspace registry. It tracks folders and GitHub-backed repositories that agents can use as context, along with metadata that makes future dispatches safer and easier to aim.

## Use it for

- Add or import repositories before asking agents to work on them.
- Set descriptions, tags, branch context, and automation preferences that explain how a workspace should be handled.
- Review git status and prepare operator-approved git actions without turning the Control Center into an invisible committer.

## Current guardrails

Workspace sync is explicit, additive, and reviewed before it scans. Pull and commit/push now show a review prompt with folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, untracked files, and the commit subject before changing files or pushing. Commit exits early when the preview sees no working changes. Project metadata save, status, delete, duplicate combine, repo create/link metadata, git actions, open-folder, and auto-commit mode changes fresh-read the tracker row before writing or using its path. Duplicate combine recomputes the duplicate groups from a fresh list and rechecks before save/remove. Auto-commit remains opt-in per project, throttled, and safe-state only: background checkpoints run only when the project row is unchanged, the repo has a linked remote, known compare state, no upstream-gone branch, zero ahead commits, zero behind commits, and working changes. Otherwise the checkpoint is skipped and the operator can review/request the commit manually.

## Good practice

Choose or update the project before sending code work through Chat or Work. A clear project focus reduces ambiguity for agents and makes attachments, uploads, and generated plans land in the expected workspace.

pages.body:
# Health

Health is the live fleet and usage view. It shows running state, token and model usage, provider activity, optional Headroom pilot readiness/policy, and the full agent roster through AgentTable.

## Use it for

- Confirm which agents are running, stopped, busy, or missing from the active fleet.
- Probe agent liveness, start or stop workers, rebuild where supported, and inspect team membership.
- Compare current token usage and model/runtime behavior before changing staffing or routing.
- Check whether the optional local Headroom CLI/proxy is ready before choosing a pilot route.
- Draft pilot guardrails before a canary: Enable pilot route, Sampling, Workspace state, and the compact Trust & routing summary. Raw route/proxy/state path, Safety mode, protected categories, and validation gates live under Advanced.

## Current guardrails

Team lead, start, reset session, stop, rebuild, probe, and delete actions fresh-read the all-team fleet row before they mutate or probe the manager, then show current status plus before/after or effect summaries where the action changes process state or routing. Runtime, model, effort, and speed dropdowns stage local draft changes instead of writing immediately; Apply changes reviews the full diff, verifies the live row still matches the staged baseline, rechecks after the confirmation prompt, blocks stale rows, and rebuilds each touched agent once. Start/Rebuild is blocked while that agent has staged config so old saved config cannot launch underneath the draft. The Headroom pilot card stages route, sampling, state, state-root, and safety changes locally; Apply policy reviews the diff before writing local pilot policy, and Refresh asks before discarding unsaved edits.

## Boundary

Health is optimized for operational status from the store snapshot and all-team aggregate poll. It is the current home for the roster grid that older specs placed on Dashboard, but it is not a long-term analytics warehouse. The Headroom card stores local pilot policy only; it does not install Headroom, start a proxy, attach MCP servers, wrap agents, or promote compressed summaries into Brain facts.

pages.body:
# Identity & Keys

Identity & Keys is the review surface for agent identity, wallet posture, Safe-style account state, and scoped session keys. It helps operators understand who can act and what account or key material is associated with that action.

## Use it for

- Select an agent from the all-team roster and inspect identity status, account details, session keys, and warnings.
- Confirm wallet-bearing agents are visible even when they belong to a team other than the manager's active team.
- Review delegated capability posture before relying on an agent for identity-sensitive work.
- Keep onchain identity, wallet controller address, Safe account, and session-key concepts visible in one place.

## Wallet source

The page treats a valid controller wallet address as active identity evidence when it appears in OWS address metadata, SkillMesh address metadata, or an address-form OWS wallet field. Register and wallet-provision actions are routed through the selected agent's team so cross-team agents are resolved against the correct manager scope.

## Current guardrails

Renderer selection, proof state, backend key-provider account IDs, Safe/session actions, and the Tauri local mock key store are keyed by team/name. A controller challenge also includes the team in the signed message and cache key, so same-named agents in different teams cannot share a privileged proof. New challenge, verify signature, provision wallet, register identity, create account, deploy account, and issue session key fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before acting; revoke also fresh-reads the latest session row and blocks if the key changed or was already revoked. The UI only issues finite, spend-capped session keys and blocks full, uncapped, or non-expiring grants. When old bare-name mock accounts or sessions match current agents, the page now shows a Legacy Authority Review card instead of silently promoting or hiding them.

## Safety note

Existing bare-name mock accounts are not automatically promoted into team-scoped accounts; review, recreate, revoke, copy, or delete them only through an explicit operator policy. Some provider behavior still depends on the active manager backend. Treat this page as the UI contract and verify the live provider before documenting stronger guarantees about signing, custody, non-expiring keys, or production-grade authorization.

pages.body:
# HR Manager

HR Manager is the organization design and routing workspace. It covers team structure, agent creation, roster management, instructions, and outbound relay topology.

## Owner

The legal-team `hr-manager` agent owns this page's staffing workflows, instruction-drafting behavior, and optimization proposals. Escalate legal-team policy or personnel-process questions through `legal/general-counsel`.

## Tab guide

- Structure: inspect the team graph and leadership shape.
- Build: draft and create agent rosters.
- Manage: edit deployed agents and their instructions.
- Route: set team coordinators and review how the default primary delegates across the fleet.

## Current guardrails

Coordinator, primary lead, team relay, and per-agent relay writes fresh-read the relevant roster, hierarchy, or policy row before showing concrete before/after routing diffs. Primary lead promotion is locked to the default team; non-default teams can still set coordinators, but their controls cannot overwrite fleet-primary authority. Instruction Save & rebuild fresh-reads the selected agent and the current saved instruction text before writing, so a stale editor cannot overwrite a newer directive or rebuild the wrong moved agent. Selected-agent rebuild and move controls re-resolve the current team/agent row before acting, and empty-team delete rechecks that the team still exists and still has no agents.

Team lifecycle now uses one consistent current-state confirmation for Start, Stop, and Rebuild: it rechecks the team roster, shows current running/total counts, and blocks missing or empty teams. Team Builder fresh-reads the target team roster before onboarding; after agents are created it rechecks hierarchy and relay stamps before automatic team-coordinator wiring or relay policy writes, and never promotes a non-default team into the fleet-primary slot. Builder coordinator presets also tell team leads to return substantial completed work through `default/coder` and `default/researcher` validation before `default/lead` treats it as final. Org sync also previews before apply; it fresh-reads org config and hierarchy, uses the same sidecar change detection as the real sync, shows scanned agents, goal files that would change, whether the brain hierarchy will be written, planned idle rebuilds under the rebuild cap, deferred rebuilds, and sample affected team/agent rows, then blocks apply if org state changed after preview. Query-busy and task-busy rebuild deferrals are team-aware where manager data includes team names, so same-named agents in different teams are less likely to block each other.

## Operating rule

Changes here affect staffing, instructions, delegation paths, and agent process state. Review generated drafts before saving, syncing, or rebuilding, and use the graph as a whole-fleet planning aid rather than a view of only the active team. Agent instruction editing is team-qualified in the picker and save path, so selecting an agent from another team loads, saves, and rebuilds that exact team/agent instead of silently falling back to the active team.

pages.body:
# Capabilities

Capabilities manages the extension surface available to teams, agents, and the Brain skill graph: MCP servers, skills, and plugins. It answers whether the fleet has the tools and instructions needed for a request before work is assigned.

## Use it for

- Inspect installed MCP servers, skills, bundled plugins, and Brain skill-catalog freshness.
- Attach or review capabilities for the selected team scope.
- Sync the local SKILL.md catalog into Brain so Brain dashboard Skills/Graph views can reason over the same skill definitions.
- Confirm tool availability before assigning work that depends on a connector, library-specific instruction, or specialist workflow.

## Current guardrails

MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild now fresh-read every selected target before the confirmation prompt and again immediately before writing. The guard compares team, name, runtime, status, health, MCP metadata, and skill metadata, blocks stale or moved agents, excludes newly incompatible runtimes, and passes each resolved target's team explicitly so all-team or team-scoped actions cannot drift through the active manager team. MCP attach/detach still builds the replacement MCP list from the fresh metadata row, so a stale UI cannot erase another page's server attachment.

MCP registry Add fresh-reads the registry and treats same-name adds as an explicit replace with before/after endpoint details. MCP registry removal fresh-reads before delete, and library skill delete fresh-reads the current skill catalog before removing a skill folder. Skill creation still relies on manager-side validation and conflict rejection.

The Skills tab now displays Brain's current skill graph count when available and provides an explicit Sync Brain action. Sync Brain additively upserts local library skills into Brain's /skills/index graph and writes a shared catalog memory, but it does not delete Brain-only skill nodes or change which agents have a skill installed.

## Scope note

Team scoping happens inside this page rather than only through the global footer selector. Keep interop fields explicit: MCP, OpenAPI, A2A, and signed-write discovery are separate concerns and should not be collapsed into one generic capability claim.

pages.body:
# Computer Use

Computer Use is the supervised local desktop-control broker. It shows the live display stream, permission state, pending approval prompts, and recent computer-use activity for agents that are allowed to request screen or input actions.

## Use it for

- Bless capable agents, arm or disarm control, and supervise the live stage.
- Approve or reject pending screen/input actions.
- Use pause, disarm, or panic controls quickly when local control should stop.

## Current guardrails

Blessing is scoped to the active team. The attached MCP config receives a team/name authority, the broker token map stores that scoped authority, and the arm-time bless list checks the same value before allowing screenshots or input. Old bare-name tokens are blocked by scoped arming and appear as a Legacy authority review notice near Who can drive, so operators can see stale access artifacts without automatically reauthorizing them.

Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and approval decisions now fresh-read broker or roster state before continuing. Arm re-reads the current attached-agent list before and after confirmation, then arms only that current list. Bless fresh-reads the selected agent row and MCP metadata before and after confirmation, blocks moved or changed targets, and re-syncs the armed blessed list from current attachments after a successful rebuild. Remove re-reads the attached row before and after confirmation, detaches only the current scoped authority, rebuilds, and refreshes the armed list if needed. Allow/Deny re-reads the pending request and blocks stale or expired approval prompts. Disabling supervised per-action approval fresh-reads broker mode and still requires confirmation.

Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths and are not blocked by long attach/rebuild work. PANIC still disarms, clears pending approvals, releases held input, and records the stop in the broker audit.

## Safety boundary

Actions should run only through the armed broker and approval flow. The page depends on local OS permissions such as Screen Recording and Accessibility. Navigating away stops the live renderer view, although global notifications can still report relevant broker state. Conservative default: Arm with no blessed agents is still allowed after confirmation for live-view or permission testing, but a stricter operator policy could require at least one blessed agent before arming.

pages.body:
# Settings

Settings contains local desktop configuration for hardware, manager connection, coordinator defaults, self-update, subscriptions, Ollama, image services, local stacks, EVM data RPCs, and inference backends.

## Use it for

- Fix manager connectivity or confirm the manager URL used by the app.
- Review subscription CLI state, local model providers, image generation services, EVM data endpoints, and inference defaults.
- Check for, stage, and relaunch into app updates.

## Current guardrails

Coordinator changes confirm before they change live team routing. Provider default, enable/disable, remove, connect/sync, same-name add/replace, local-discovery add, and Add all new now fresh-read the current provider list before writing. Disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked before and after confirmation. Same-name provider replacement shows before/after endpoint details, and discovered-server bulk add re-runs discovery and de-dupes against the fresh provider list before adding anything.

EVM RPC save, replace, remove, and check fresh-read the current endpoint row before acting; replacing an existing endpoint shows before/after URLs. Local Ollama auto-backend creation rechecks current providers before adding the default Ollama backend. Local concurrency Apply fresh-reads the manager's current local inference limit before writing, and image-server Save/Clear fresh-reads the current preferred local image generator before changing local-vs-cloud routing. Local LLM stacks now sort starter-friendly choices first and label install effort as start here, easy, guided, or advanced; Terminal install commands remain reviewed and explicit, with visible clipboard fallback when automation is blocked. Sign-out, stack install/uninstall, model removal, and provider removal remain explicit operator actions.

## Boundary

These settings are app-local configuration and infrastructure routing, not manager task data. Because release and update behavior is surfaced here, keep this page aligned with the package version and release process; the wiki drift check exists to catch stale release-facing documentation before shipping.

pages.purpose: Current audit record for controls that can rewrite live agent, task, project, capability, identity, or provider state and therefore need review, confirmation, or stronger state synchronization.
pages.scope: Documentation and implementation record for the current modified build. It records the first full dashboard-page audit, twenty-eight conservative guardrail implementation passes, and the policy decisions still needed before more disruptive UX simplification.

tabs.label: Dashboard
tabs.description: Dashboard is now observe-first by default: command-palette project/org sync entries route to owner pages, the shortcuts drawer shows observe and owner-page handoffs first, direct project/org shortcuts sit behind Advanced, direct org sync uses org:preview, and primary-lead changes move to HR Manager.

tabs.label: Work
tabs.description: Task lane drag/drop, owner reassignment, and reconcile/redispatch now fresh-read current task status, owner, team, and lane before writing. Plan work/status, goal edit/refine/delete, saved-loop save/run/schedule/delete, heartbeat/check-in, and dream delete paths fresh-read current records before writing. Autopilot prompts show global scope, and Dream blocks duplicate nightly schedules.

tabs.label: Projects
tabs.description: Git actions, direct commit/push, auto-commit, repo create/link/fork, and pull actions can change repository or project-tracker state. Workspace sync is explicit, commit/push and pull preview branch/remote/status/diff/untracked files, project-row writes fresh-read current tracker state, and background checkpoints only run when project and branch/remote state are exactly safe.

tabs.label: Health
tabs.description: Runtime/model/effort/speed selectors stage local drafts and apply only after full diff review plus fresh fleet-row checks before and after confirmation. Team lead, start, reset session, stop, rebuild, probe, and delete fresh-read the current row before changing context, process state, team routing, or liveness state. Headroom pilot policy edits are staged until Apply policy reviews the diff.

tabs.label: Identity & Keys
tabs.description: Controller proof gates privileged actions; renderer selection, proof cache, backend account IDs, Safe/session actions, and the Tauri local mock key store are team/name keyed. Identity actions fresh-read the selected agent row, revoke fresh-reads the latest session row, and legacy bare-name key records surface as review-only evidence instead of being auto-promoted.

tabs.label: HR Manager
tabs.description: Coordinator changes, default-only primary lead promotion, relay overrides, instruction saves with rebuild, Team Builder auto-wiring, selected-agent rebuild, team lifecycle, empty-team delete, and org sync fresh-read current roster/config/hierarchy state before writing or rebuilding. Team Builder coordinator presets now include the default coder/researcher validation return path.

tabs.label: Capabilities
tabs.description: MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and rebuild fresh-read selected targets before and after confirmation; registry add/remove and skill delete fresh-read current catalogs before replacing or removing records. The Skills tab now shows Brain skill-index status and has an explicit additive Sync Brain action for local SKILL.md catalog drift.

tabs.label: Computer Use
tabs.description: Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and Allow/Deny now fresh-read broker, pending-approval, attached-agent, or roster state before continuing. Tokens, attached-agent rows, and the arm bless-list use team/name scoped authority; legacy bare-name tokens surface as review-only evidence and stay blocked by scoped arming.

tabs.label: Settings
tabs.description: Provider default/toggle/remove/connect/add, local discovery adds, EVM RPC writes, local concurrency, and image-server preference changes now fresh-read current settings before writing. Coordinator selection confirms, and the last enabled inference backend cannot be disabled or removed.

controls.label: Preview before apply
controls.description: Use for org sync, git commit/push, capability bulk apply, and hierarchy/routing changes. The preview should show target agents/teams, files or repos touched, and whether a rebuild or push will happen.

controls.label: Stage then apply
controls.description: Use for compact dropdown-heavy config surfaces such as Health runtime/model/effort/speed. Draft changes locally, review the whole diff, block stale rows, and rebuild or write once per touched target.

controls.label: Fresh task preflight
controls.description: Use for task-card actions that mutate manager status, owner, or background dispatch. Refetch the task list plus lane/dependency overlays, block when status/owner/team/lane changed, and refresh before the operator tries again.

controls.label: Fresh plan preflight
controls.description: Use for brain-plan status writes, blocker-question creation, dispatch, and draft-plan promotion or deletion. Refetch live plan title/status/mtime or draft version/updatedAt/content before applying the action, and have the main writer reject stale expected brain-plan status snapshots.

controls.label: Fresh goal preflight
controls.description: Use for saved goal edits, AI refine saves, deletion, and driver metadata writes. Refetch the goal updatedAt marker before saving from an opened editor, and merge background driver metadata onto the latest saved goal instead of an older loaded copy.

controls.label: Fresh loop preflight
controls.description: Use for saved sequential loop chains. Keep the opened loop's updatedAt baseline, block save/run/schedule/delete when the saved chain changed, refresh the editor to the latest chain, and reload the saved record after each save so the baseline stays aligned.

controls.label: Fresh schedule preflight
controls.description: Use for heartbeat, schedule, check-in, and dream-schedule controls. Refetch current schedules or check-ins before update/pause/remove/close, block stale rendered rows, and reject duplicate recurring Dream schedules before creating another calendar check-in.

controls.label: Fresh project preflight
controls.description: Use for project tracker writes and filesystem/git actions. Capture the opened project row, refetch before saving status/metadata/delete/auto-commit, re-anchor git actions to the current path, and recompute duplicate groups before bulk save/remove.

controls.label: Fresh agent preflight
controls.description: Use for Health roster actions and staged agent config writes. Refetch the all-team fleet row before lifecycle, probe, lead, delete, and Apply actions; block if name/team/status/runtime/model/effort/speed/health/port changed before writing.

controls.label: Fresh identity preflight
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys privileged actions. Refetch the selected team/agent row before challenge, verify, wallet, account, deploy, and issue actions; refetch the latest account/session row before revoke so stale session state cannot disable the wrong grant.

controls.label: Fresh HR preflight
controls.description: Use for HR Manager staffing and routing writes. Refetch the target roster, hierarchy, relay policy, org config, or instruction text before coordinator/default-primary changes, relay overrides, instruction save+rebuild, team lifecycle, Team Builder post-build team-coordinator wiring, empty-team delete, and org sync apply.

controls.label: Fresh capability preflight
controls.description: Use for Capabilities target writes. Refetch every selected team/agent before and after confirmation, block moved or newly incompatible agents, compare current MCP/skill metadata, and pass each resolved target team explicitly before MCP, skill, or rebuild writes.

controls.label: Fresh Computer Use preflight
controls.description: Use for Computer Use Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode, and approval decisions. Refetch broker status, pending requests, attached agents, and selected roster rows before acting; recheck after confirmation when authority or rebuild state can change.

controls.label: Fresh Settings preflight
controls.description: Use for Settings provider, RPC, local concurrency, and image-server writes. Refetch the current settings row or manager value before writing, recheck after confirmation, and block stale replacement or last-enabled-backend mutations.

controls.label: Fresh read before full-list write
controls.description: Use for MCP server attach/detach and any API that posts a complete list. Refetch current agent metadata immediately before constructing the next list so stale UI state cannot erase another page's change.

controls.label: Team/id scoped authority
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys and Computer Use so accounts, proofs, session grants, broker tokens, and blessed authority cannot collide when teams contain same-named agents. Legacy bare-name records should surface as review evidence until an explicit migration policy exists.

controls.label: Legacy authority review
controls.description: Use when older unscoped accounts, sessions, tokens, or permissions still exist. Show them as stale review artifacts, never as current authority, and require an operator policy before copying, revoking, or deleting them.

controls.label: Two-step destructive actions
controls.description: Use for reset session, rebuild active agents, provider removal, disabling the last usable backend, disabling Computer Use supervised mode, and stopping/removing recurring schedules.

controls.label: Observe-first Dashboard
controls.description: Keep Dashboard focused on observe, talk, refresh, and status. Move high-impact mutators such as primary-lead changes and org sync to HR Manager or require an explicit confirmation flow.

controls.label: Additive Brain catalog sync
controls.description: Use when an IDACC catalog feeds a Brain dashboard graph. Show the Brain count, make sync explicit, upsert current local definitions, and avoid deleting Brain-only learned nodes during refresh.

Projects: auto-commit remains opt-in per project and the automated checkpoint path reuses the reviewed enablement guardrail instead of prompting mid-background run.

Capabilities: default scope is now this team, broad or multi-agent applies require confirmation, MCP attach/detach refetches fresh agent metadata before posting a replacement list, and MCP registry removal confirms.
Computer Use: disabling supervised per-action approval now requires confirmation.
Settings: provider default changes, disabling enabled providers, and provider removal now require confirmation.
implementedSecondPass: Dashboard: drawer and command-palette project/org sync actions now confirm; primary lead selection routes by team/name and confirms before org sync.
Work: task lane status writes, owner reassignment, reconcile, redispatch, plan work/status changes, goal autopilot, schedules, loops, and dreams now confirm before live writes or dispatch.
Health: team lead, model/runtime/effort/speed, reset session, stop, and rebuild actions now confirm, with explicit active-agent warnings.

Identity & Keys: selected agent and controller proof state are team/name keyed in the renderer; wallet/account/session issue/deploy/revoke actions now confirm in addition to existing controller proof gates.

HR Manager: coordinator/primary changes, relay policy writes, per-agent relay overrides, instruction save+rebuild, selected-agent rebuild, Team Builder post-build wiring, org auto-sync toggles, and org sync now confirm.
implementedThirdPass: Identity & Keys: controller challenge messages, proof cache keys, key-provider account IDs, Safe/session deploy/issue/revoke calls, and the Tauri local mock key store now use team/name scoped authority.

Computer Use: attach/detach/attached methods route through the selected team, the MCP env carries ID_CU_TEAM and team/name ID_CU_AGENT authority, broker tokens store scoped authorities, and arm checks the same scoped bless-list.
Settings: coordinator changes now confirm, and disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked.
implementedFourthPass: HR Manager: Org sync now has an org:preview dry-run that uses the same sidecar change detection as org:sync and reports scanned agents, changed goal sidecars, planned brain write, planned rebuilds, rebuild cap, deferred rebuilds, and affected team/agent rows before apply.
HR Manager: Org sync task-busy and query-busy rebuild deferrals are now scoped by team where manager data exposes team names, reducing same-name cross-team rebuild ambiguity.
implementedFifthPass: Projects: manual commit/push now previews folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, untracked files, and commit subject before staging, committing, or pushing.
Projects: pull now previews folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, and untracked files before running the resilient pull.
Projects: manual commit exits early when the preview sees no working changes, avoiding a no-op commit attempt.
implementedSixthPass: HR Manager: coordinator changes now preview the previous coordinator and current primary before writing.
HR Manager: primary lead promotion now previews old primary, new primary, and affected team coordinator before writing.
HR Manager: team relay policy and per-agent relay overrides now preview previous and next delegation policy before writing.
implementedSeventhPass: Health: fleet lead, model, runtime, effort, speed, reset-session, stop, and rebuild confirmations now include current agent status plus before/after fields or effect summaries.

Health: Headroom pilot route, sampling, context threshold, workspace state, state-root, and safety-mode edits are staged locally; Apply policy previews the diff before writing, and Refresh asks before discarding unsaved policy edits.
Health: the Tauri adapter now honors the selected row's team for remote, probe, runtime, effort, and speed calls so all-team roster actions do not fall back to the active team.
implementedEighthPass: Dashboard: command-palette project/org sync commands now open Projects or HR Manager instead of mutating tracker or org state directly.
Dashboard: shortcuts drawer now defaults to observe actions and owner-page handoffs; direct project/org shortcuts live behind Advanced direct shortcuts.
Dashboard: direct org sync reuses org:preview before apply, and primary fleet-lead changes are no longer exposed as a Dashboard selector because HR Manager owns the routing diff preview.
implementedNinthPass: Projects: background auto-commit checkpoints now preflight git status and diff before drafting or committing.

Projects: auto-commit skips to manual review when the repo has no remote, unknown compare state, an upstream-gone branch, any behind commits, any ahead commits, unavailable diff data, or no working changes.
Projects: auto-commit enablement copy and tooltip now state that checkpoints only commit and push when the branch is exactly in sync with its remote.
implementedTenthPass: Identity & Keys: mock key providers now expose a read-only legacy authority report for old bare-name accounts and sessions that match current scoped agents.

Identity & Keys: the renderer shows a Legacy Authority Review card with account, deployment, active-session, non-expiring-session, and target scoped-authority details instead of silently promoting legacy key records.
Computer Use: the broker now exposes a secret-free legacy token report for old bare-name per-agent tokens that match current scoped agents.
Computer Use: the Who can drive panel now surfaces old bare-name tokens as blocked-by-scoped-arming review evidence instead of treating them as active authority.
implementedEleventhPass: Health: runtime, model, effort, and speed dropdowns now stage local config drafts instead of writing immediately.
Health: Apply changes previews the combined diff, verifies the live agent row still matches the staged name/status/runtime/model/effort/speed baseline, and blocks stale drafts before any write.
Health: applying staged config writes each changed field and rebuilds each touched agent once; Start/Rebuild is blocked while that agent has an unapplied config draft.
implementedTwelfthPass: Work: task lane drag/drop now refetches live tasks, lane overlays, and dependency overlays before writing a lane overlay or mapped manager status, blocking stale status/owner/team/lane cards.
Work: owner assignment now refetches the task and blocks if live status, owner, or team changed before overwriting ownership.
Work: individual and batch re-dispatch now fresh-read the task before reassigning or sending a new background request; stale batch rows are skipped and the board refreshes.
implementedThirteenthPass: Work Plans: Work, blocker scan, dispatch, Set pending, and Mark done fresh-read live brain-plan title/status/mtime before writing status, creating decision questions, or delegating work.
Work Plans: the main brain-plan status writer accepts an expected status/mtime snapshot and rejects stale writes if README status or plan-file mtime changed after review.
Work Plans: draft plan AI updates, promotion into brain plans, lifecycle status changes, and two-step delete now preflight the opened draft version/updatedAt/content before saving or removing.
implementedFourteenthPass: Work Goals: field edits, status changes, Autopilot toggles, AI refine saves, and two-step delete now refetch the opened goal updatedAt marker and block if the saved goal changed.
Work Goals: goal-driver config changes merge with freshly loaded driver config, and Autopilot enable/run-now prompts show the current global active Autopilot goal count and task cap.
Goal driver: background driver metadata saves now merge onto the latest saved goal record so driver notes cannot restore stale title/status/content from the copy loaded at run start.
Work Goals: removed the duplicate New Goal agent label so the form presents one clear agent row.
implementedFifteenthPass: Work Goals: after goal save/refine/field edit, the editor reloads the saved goal record so its freshness marker matches the store-written updatedAt value.
Work Loops: opening a saved chain now records a baseline and save/run/schedule operations block if that saved chain changed before the action applies.
Work Loops: run and schedule persist run metadata or schedule-ready definitions onto the freshly checked chain instead of an older opened copy.
Work Loops: saved-chain delete checks the rendered updatedAt marker and refreshes instead of deleting a chain that changed after the list rendered.
implementedSixteenthPass: Work Schedule: heartbeat enable/update now refetches current heartbeat rows before creating the replacement and pruning old schedules.
Work Schedule: heartbeat pause/resume/remove and other-team heartbeat controls refetch the schedule row and block if active state, interval, targets, or message changed.
Work Schedule: individual check-in close and stale-check-in cleanup refetch current check-in state and block when the active/stale set changed.
Work Dream: nightly dream scheduling checks for an existing 03:00 daily dream schedule for the same team/agent and passes the team explicitly when creating one.
Work Dream: dream report delete refetches the report and blocks if it disappeared or changed since the list rendered.
implementedSeventeenthPass: Projects: edit/save captures the opened project baseline, fresh-reads the tracker row before saving metadata, and preserves existing auto-commit settings during edits.
Projects: status changes, two-step delete, and auto-commit mode changes now refetch the current project row before writing.
Projects: manual commit, AI commit drafting, git actions, open-folder, and background auto-commit checkpoints re-anchor to the current project path before touching the filesystem.
Projects: repo create/link checks the project row before the GitHub side effect, then merges the resulting repo link into the latest tracker row instead of overwriting newer metadata.
Projects: duplicate combine recomputes duplicate groups from a fresh list, previews the group names, and rechecks the group fingerprint before save/remove.
implementedEighteenthPass: Health: staged runtime/model/effort/speed Apply now refetches the all-team fleet before the review prompt and again immediately before writing.
Health: team lead changes refetch the agent row and current coordinator hierarchy before confirming and writing coordinator routing.
Health: Start, Stop, Rebuild, Reset session, Probe, and Delete refetch the current agent row before issuing remote lifecycle commands.
Health: Start now confirms like the other lifecycle mutators, and Delete uses the richer status/effect confirmation instead of a bare one-line prompt.
Health: direct Probe buttons now route through the same fresh-agent preflight as the action menu.
implementedNineteenthPass: Identity & Keys: New challenge and Verify signature now fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before using controller wallet evidence.
Identity & Keys: Provision wallet, Register identity, Create account, Deploy account, and Issue scoped key fresh-read the selected agent row before mutating key or identity state.
Identity & Keys: Deploy also refreshes account state and blocks if the account was already deployed after the card rendered.
Identity & Keys: Revoke session key fresh-reads both selected agent state and the latest session row, blocking if the grant disappeared, changed, or was already revoked.
Identity & Keys: privileged button handlers now route through the guarded action functions instead of using stale selected name/team arguments captured from the rendered card.
implementedTwentiethPass: HR Manager: instruction Save & rebuild fresh-reads the target team/agent row and the current saved instruction text before writing, then rebuilds only that current agent.
HR Manager: selected-agent rebuild, move, empty-team delete, team relay, and per-agent relay controls fresh-read the relevant roster or policy row before prompting and writing.
HR Manager: coordinator and primary-lead changes fresh-read current hierarchy plus target agent existence before showing the routing diff prompt.
HR Manager: team lifecycle Start, Stop, and Rebuild now use one current-state confirmation with fresh running/total counts, and block missing or empty teams.
HR Manager: Team Builder fresh-reads the target team roster before onboarding and rechecks hierarchy/relay stamps before post-build coordinator or relay auto-wiring.
HR Manager: org auto-sync toggles and Preview & sync fresh-read org config and hierarchy; sync apply blocks if org state changed after preview.
implementedTwentyFirstPass: Capabilities: MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild now fresh-read every selected target before the confirmation prompt and again immediately before writing.
Capabilities: target preflight blocks moved agents, newly incompatible runtimes, and stale MCP/skill metadata, then passes each resolved target team explicitly to manager methods.
Capabilities: MCP attach/detach still constructs the replacement MCP server list from the fresh metadata row so wholesale MCP writes cannot erase newer attachments.
Capabilities: adding an MCP profile with an existing name now fresh-reads the registry and requires an explicit replace confirmation with before/after endpoint details.
Capabilities: MCP registry removal and library skill deletion fresh-read current catalogs before removing records.
implementedTwentySecondPass: Computer Use: Arm fresh-reads broker status and current attached agents before and after confirmation, blocking if the broker was armed or the blessed list changed during review.

Computer Use: Bless fresh-reads the selected active-team agent row and MCP metadata before and after confirmation, blocks moved/changed/incompatible targets, and re-syncs the armed blessed list from current attachments after a successful rebuild.

Computer Use: Remove fresh-reads the attached scoped authority before and after confirmation, detaches only the current row, rebuilds, and refreshes the armed blessed list if the broker is already armed.
Computer Use: Allow/Deny fresh-reads the pending request and blocks stale, changed, expired, or already-resolved approvals before releasing an action.
Computer Use: Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths and are no longer disabled by long attach/rebuild work; supervised-mode changes fresh-read broker mode before writing.
implementedTwentyThirdPass: Settings: provider default, enable/disable, remove, connect/sync, and same-name add/replace fresh-read the current provider row before writing and recheck after confirmation where routing changes.
Settings: disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked using the fresh provider list before and after confirmation.
Settings: discovered local-server Add and Add all new re-read the provider list, re-run discovery for bulk add, de-dupe by normalized base URL, and confirm the current bulk-add target list.
Settings: EVM RPC save/replace/remove/check fresh-read the current endpoint row before writing or probing, with before/after URL review for replacement.

Settings: local concurrency Apply fresh-reads the manager's current limit, image-server Save/Clear fresh-reads the current preference before changing local-vs-cloud routing, and Ollama auto-backend creation rechecks current providers before adding.
implementedTwentyFourthPass: Brain dashboard: /fleet-report now prefers live all-team manager polling over cached Brain agent entities.
Brain dashboard: Fleet and Agents tabs label live-manager versus cache fallback source and surface cache-drift warnings when Brain memory counts disagree with the live manager.
Brain dashboard: regression coverage verifies that live manager totals win over stale cached agent entities.
implementedTwentyFifthPass: HR Manager: fleet-primary promotion is locked to the default team in Structure and Route controls, while non-default teams can still choose team coordinators.

HR Manager: Team Builder post-build auto-wiring now calls coordinator:set for the target team instead of coordinator:setPrimary, so building an ops/research/engineering team cannot overwrite default-team primary authority.
HR Manager: Reactive org-sync copy now describes completed work flowing back through default-team validators, coder and researcher, before returning to the primary lead.
implementedTwentySixthPass: HR Manager: generic and roster-aware Team Builder coordinator presets now include a validation return path through default/coder and default/researcher before work is treated as final.
HR Manager: coordinator presets now tell team leads to ask coder for implementation/operations/code-quality validation and researcher for evidence/reasoning/sourcing/policy/completeness validation.
HR Manager: bounced validator feedback now routes back to the responsible teammate or team lead for another refinement cycle instead of letting unvalidated summaries reach default/lead.
implementedTwentySeventhPass: Settings: Local LLM stacks now carry install-ease labels so starter paths are distinguishable from advanced serving engines.
Settings: the local-stack shortlist now orders Ollama, LM Studio, Jan, GPT4All, llama.cpp, MLX, LocalAI, Docker Model Runner, vLLM, and TGI for a download-and-drive operator path.
Settings: reviewed stack install commands now show visible Terminal-open or clipboard-fallback status instead of failing silently when Terminal automation is blocked.
recommendedFirstPass: Projects: decide whether auto-commit should remain as an explicit per-project feature or be removed from the UI.
Settings: decide whether same-name inference provider replacement should stay allowed with explicit before/after confirmation or require unique backend names.
Computer Use: decide whether Arm with no blessed agents should stay allowed after confirmation for live-view permission testing or be blocked until at least one agent is blessed.
Identity and Computer Use: decide whether legacy bare-name accounts/tokens should stay manual-review only or get an explicit migration assistant.
Identity and Computer Use: consider upgrading from team/name authority to immutable team/id authority if the manager exposes stable cross-team IDs for every shell.
routing.releaseState: This wiki page documents the current twenty-seventh guardrail pass and should ship with the next IDACC release after typecheck and wiki validation pass.

notes: Existing protections already found: delete task, delete project, combine duplicate projects, sign out, RPC removal, model removal, stack install/uninstall, team stop/rebuild, empty-team delete, Identity privileged actions, and Computer Use broker actions have confirmations or gates.
Most dangerous stale-write class found: UI computes a replacement state from a snapshot and sends a wholesale write, especially MCP server lists and git/project automation.

Most dangerous background automation found: project auto-commit can commit and push after task completion without another user click; the modified build makes enabling that behavior explicit per project.
Third guardrail pass closes same-name authority collisions for Identity & Keys and Computer Use without auto-migrating legacy bare-name records.
Fourth guardrail pass adds preview-before-apply to HR org sync and scopes org-sync busy checks by team where data is available.
Fifth guardrail pass adds preview-before-apply to Projects pull and manual commit/push.
Sixth guardrail pass adds before/after previews for HR hierarchy and relay routing writes.
Seventh guardrail pass adds richer Health agent-change previews, stages Headroom pilot policy writes behind Apply policy, and keeps Tauri all-team roster actions team-scoped.

Eighth guardrail pass makes Dashboard observe-first by routing palette mutations to owner pages, hiding direct sync shortcuts behind Advanced, requiring org preview from Dashboard, and moving primary-lead changes to HR Manager.
Ninth guardrail pass makes Projects auto-commit safe-state only and skips background checkpoints when branch/remote state is ambiguous or not exactly in sync.
Tenth guardrail pass surfaces legacy bare-name Identity key records and Computer Use tokens as review-only artifacts while keeping scoped team/name authority as the active path.
Eleventh guardrail pass stages Health runtime/model/effort/speed changes behind one Apply flow with stale-row checks and one rebuild per touched agent.
Twelfth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work task cards before lane, owner, or re-dispatch writes so stale board state cannot overwrite current task state.
Thirteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work plan records and adds backend expected-state rejection for brain-plan status writes.
Fourteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work goal records before goal edits/refine/delete and keeps goal-driver metadata saves from clobbering newer goal fields.
Fifteenth guardrail pass keeps Work goal freshness markers aligned after saves and fresh-reads saved loop chains before save/run/schedule/delete actions.
Sixteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work schedule/check-in rows before heartbeat or supervision mutations and prevents duplicate nightly Dream schedules.
Seventeenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Projects tracker rows before metadata, status, delete, git, repo-link, duplicate-combine, and auto-commit writes.
Eighteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Health fleet rows before staged config, lifecycle, lead, probe, and delete actions.
Nineteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Identity selected-agent and session-key rows before controller, wallet, account, deploy, issue, and revoke actions.
Twentieth guardrail pass fresh-reads HR Manager roster, hierarchy, relay, instruction, org config, and builder target state before staffing, routing, lifecycle, rebuild, and org-sync writes.
Twenty-first guardrail pass fresh-reads Capabilities targets and catalogs before MCP, skill, rebuild, registry replace, registry remove, and skill delete actions.
Twenty-second guardrail pass fresh-reads Computer Use broker, roster, attached-agent, and pending-approval state before arm, bless, remove, approval, and supervised-mode writes.
Twenty-third guardrail pass fresh-reads Settings provider, EVM RPC, local concurrency, image-server, discovered-server, and Ollama auto-backend state before infrastructure-routing writes.
Twenty-fourth guardrail pass makes the external Brain dashboard prefer live all-team manager fleet state over cached Brain agent entities, surfacing cache drift when the two disagree.
Twenty-fifth guardrail pass locks HR Manager fleet-primary promotion to the default team and changes Team Builder post-build wiring to set only the target team's coordinator.

Twenty-sixth guardrail pass updates HR Manager Team Builder coordinator presets so completed work returns through default/coder and default/researcher validation before default/lead receives final findings.
Twenty-seventh guardrail pass makes Settings local-model stack installation easier to reason about by prioritizing starter paths, labeling install effort, and surfacing Terminal fallback status.
Twenty-eighth guardrail pass makes the Capabilities Skills tab a Brain-aware skill catalog with a visible Brain graph count and an explicit additive Sync Brain action.
The guardrail passes favor confirmation prompts over moving controls; the open UX decision is whether to replace confirmed shortcuts with preview-and-apply flows.
Most important simplification: separate status/observation pages from mutation/control surfaces wherever possible.
pages.body:
# Sync & Guardrails Audit

This page records the full pass over the dashboard pages in order: Dashboard, Work, Projects, Health, Identity & Keys, HR Manager, Capabilities, Computer Use, and Settings. It now documents the audit findings plus twenty-eight conservative guardrail implementation passes.

## Current build state

The dashboard family is sync-bus aware: dashboard, work, projects, health, brain-backed plans/goals/loops/schedules/dreams, agent capability changes, and org sync emit coarse domain invalidations so mounted pages refresh together after relevant mutations. The guardrail layer now reduces stale task-card writes, stale plan status/dispatch writes, stale goal edits, stale loop chain writes, stale schedule/check-in writes, stale project tracker writes, stale Health fleet-row writes, stale Identity selected-agent/session writes, stale HR roster/hierarchy/relay/instruction/org-sync writes, stale Capabilities target/catalog writes, stale Computer Use arm/bless/remove/approval writes, stale Settings provider/RPC/concurrency/image-server writes, surprise mutations, same-name cross-team authority collisions, hidden legacy authority artifacts, unreviewed org-sync sidecar/rebuild changes, unreviewed repository mutations, opaque hierarchy/routing edits, staged Health config drift, Dashboard shortcut mutations, unsafe background auto-commit states, Brain-dashboard cached fleet drift, non-default HR primary-lead overwrites, stale team-lead coordinator presets, flat local-model install choices, and stale IDACC-to-Brain skill catalog drift without removing the existing owner-page workflows.

## Implemented guardrails

- Dashboard is observe-first by default: command-palette project/org sync entries open Projects or HR Manager, the shortcuts drawer shows observe actions and owner-page handoffs first, direct project/org shortcuts live behind Advanced, direct org sync previews affected agents/goals/brain/rebuilds before apply, and primary-lead changes now belong to HR Manager's routing diff preview.
- Brain dashboard fleet and agents tabs now treat the live manager as authoritative for current status: /fleet-report follows IDACC's all-team polling shape, reports source/live-vs-cache drift, and labels Brain entity cache fallback instead of presenting stale memory as current fleet state.
- Work confirms task status writes, owner reassignment, reconcile, redispatch, plan work/status writes, goal autopilot, recurring schedules, loops, and dreams. Task lane, owner, and re-dispatch actions fresh-read live task status, owner, team, and lane before writing. Plan work, blocker scan, dispatch, status, update, promote, and delete paths fresh-read live brain/draft plan state before writing, with backend stale-snapshot rejection for brain-plan status writes. Goal edit/refine/delete paths fresh-read the saved goal updatedAt marker before writing, and goal-driver metadata saves merge onto the latest goal record. Saved loop chain save/run/schedule/delete paths block stale opened or listed loop records before writing or dispatching. Schedule heartbeat/check-in actions fresh-read rows before update/pause/remove/close, and Dream blocks duplicate nightly schedules plus stale report deletes.
- Projects no longer silently syncs the workspace on first open. Pull and manual commit/push now preview folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stats, untracked files, and commit subject where relevant. Project metadata save, status, delete, duplicate combine, repo create/link metadata, git actions, open-folder, and auto-commit mode changes fresh-read the tracker row before writing or using its path. Enabling auto-commit remains explicit per project; background auto-commit now preflights the current project row, git status, and diff, and skips to manual review unless the row is unchanged, the repo has a remote, known compare state, no upstream-gone branch, zero ahead commits, zero behind commits, and working changes.
- Health stages runtime/model/effort/speed changes locally, applies them only after a combined diff prompt, verifies the live row still matches the staged name/status/runtime/model/effort/speed baseline before and after confirmation, blocks stale drafts, and rebuilds each touched agent once. Team lead, start, reset session, stop, rebuild, probe, and delete actions fresh-read the current all-team fleet row before changing live routing, context, process state, or liveness state. Headroom pilot policy edits are staged locally and written only through Apply policy after a diff prompt. Tauri all-team roster actions preserve the selected row's team for remote, probe, runtime, effort, and speed calls.
- Identity & Keys now keys renderer selection, controller proof cache, backend account IDs, Safe/session actions, and Tauri mock key state by team/name, with controller proof gates and capped finite session-key enforcement still in place. Controller challenge/verify, wallet provisioning, identity registration, account creation/deploy, and session issue fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before acting; revoke also fresh-reads the latest session row before disabling a grant. Old bare-name mock accounts and sessions now appear in a Legacy Authority Review card instead of being silently promoted or hidden.
- HR Manager now fresh-reads before staffing, routing, instruction, lifecycle, rebuild, Team Builder post-build, and org-sync writes. Coordinator and default-only primary changes check current hierarchy and agent existence before previewing diffs; non-default teams can set coordinators but cannot promote themselves into fleet-primary authority. Team and per-agent relay writes check current relay policy or override before saving; instruction Save & rebuild checks current agent state and saved instruction text; team lifecycle Start/Stop/Rebuild shows fresh running/total counts; Team Builder checks the target roster before onboarding, rechecks hierarchy/relay stamps before team-coordinator auto-wiring, and writes coordinator presets that return completed work through default/coder and default/researcher validation before default/lead gets final findings; org sync checks org config and hierarchy before preview and blocks apply if either changed after preview.
- Capabilities now fresh-reads every selected target before and after MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild confirmation; moved agents, newly incompatible runtimes, or changed MCP/skill metadata block the write. MCP attach/detach still constructs replacement MCP lists from fresh metadata and routes each action to the resolved team. MCP registry Add now confirms same-name replacement after a fresh registry read, and registry removal plus library skill delete fresh-read current catalogs before removing records. The Skills tab now shows Brain /skills/index status and can explicitly sync the local SKILL.md catalog into Brain with additive graph upserts and a shared memory summary.
- Computer Use now fresh-reads broker status and attached-agent state before and after Arm confirmation; Bless/Remove fresh-read the selected roster or attached row before and after confirmation; Allow/Deny fresh-read pending approvals before releasing an action; supervised-mode changes fresh-read broker mode before writing. Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths during long attach/rebuild work. Old bare-name per-agent tokens still appear as blocked legacy authority review evidence near Who can drive.
- Settings now fresh-reads provider rows before default/toggle/remove/connect/add replacement writes, blocks last-enabled-backend loss using the fresh list, rechecks discovered local servers before bulk add, fresh-reads EVM RPC rows before save/replace/remove/check, and guards local concurrency plus image-server preference writes against stale settings. Local stack choices now put starter-friendly options first, label install effort, keep reviewed Terminal commands explicit, and surface clipboard fallback when Terminal automation is blocked.

## Remaining hard guardrail candidates

- Projects auto-commit should either remain an explicit per-project feature or be removed from the UI.
- Settings same-name inference provider replacement should either remain allowed with explicit before/after confirmation or require unique backend names.
- Computer Use Arm with no blessed agents should either remain allowed after confirmation for live-view testing or become blocked until at least one agent is blessed.
- Legacy bare-name Identity accounts and Computer Use tokens are now surfaced; decide whether they should stay manual-review only or get a migration assistant.
- If stable immutable cross-team IDs become available everywhere, Identity and Computer Use should consider upgrading authority keys from team/name to team/id.

## UX simplification target

Dashboard should stay mostly observe, talk, and refresh. Work should stage or preview bulk redistribution and recurring behavior. Projects should separate tracker edits from repository-changing operations. HR Manager should own hierarchy and org-sync controls, with default-team primary authority kept distinct from per-team coordinator choice. Capabilities should make bulk scope obvious before applying tools across teams and keep Brain catalog sync explicit/additive. Computer Use should reserve friction for privilege expansion and stale approval risk while keeping stop controls immediate. Settings should keep first-run model setup legible by separating starter local stacks from advanced serving engines while confirming infrastructure-routing changes and blocking impossible-to-run configurations.

## Open operator decisions

- Should Capabilities allow same-name MCP registry replacement with explicit confirmation, or block replacement and require a new server name?
- Should Capabilities Sync Brain remain explicit-only, or should create/delete skill actions offer an optional follow-up prompt to refresh Brain immediately?
- Should Settings allow same-name inference provider replacement with explicit before/after confirmation, or block replacement and require a new backend name?
- Should HR Manager keep guarded Team Builder team-coordinator auto-wiring, move all coordinator wiring to a manual post-build step, or disable builder-driven routing changes entirely?
- Should HR Manager keep stamped org-sync auto-rebuild, make org sync write goals only with manual rebuilds, or disable org sync until manually re-enabled?
- Should Projects keep auto-commit as a feature if it is explicit, reviewed, and per-project, or should it be removed from the UI?
- Should Computer Use allow Arm with no blessed agents for live-view permission testing, or block Arm until at least one current agent is blessed?
- Should legacy bare-name Identity accounts and Computer Use tokens stay review-only, or should IDACC offer an explicit copy/archive/revoke/delete migration flow?

pages.body:
# Wiki

Wiki is both the human documentation page and the editable schematic for the Control Center UI. Each article explains the operator purpose of a page; the structured sections below preserve tabs, tiles, fields, controls, displays, routes, and source files for implementation detail.

## Use it for

- Search pages, controls, routes, source files, and notes from inside the desktop app.
- Confirm how a UI surface is intended to behave before editing code or filing follow-up work.
- Keep app navigation metadata and human documentation in one reviewed JSON artifact.

## Editing rule

The desktop app hot-reload polls CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json through the main-process wiki bridge. Sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility still come from pages[].nav for implemented routes, so documentation edits must preserve that metadata and keep every page.body non-empty.
